steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content:
In any triangle, the sum of the three interior angles equals 180°. With angle P = 38° and angle Q = 90°, we write:
$$38^\circ + 90^\circ + R = 180^\circ$$

steps.title: Solve for angle R
steps.content:
Simplify the left side:
$$128^\circ + R = 180^\circ$$
Subtract $128^\circ$ from both sides:
$$R = 52^\circ$$
That is the correct answer.
